steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Set up the density formula
steps.content:
Population density equals total population divided by total area:
$$\text{Density} = \frac{\text{Population}}{\text{Area}} = \frac{2{,}278}{134} \text{ muskrats per acre}$$

steps.title: Divide
steps.content:
$$\frac{2{,}278}{134} = 17$$
Check: $134 \times 17 = 134 \times 10 + 134 \times 7 = 1{,}340 + 938 = 2{,}278.$ ✓

The estimated population density is 17 muskrats per acre.
